articles.description:
Subway adds a trio of Thanksgiving subs to the menu with the introduction of its new, limited-time Festive Feast collection.

Available nationwide on November 13, 2025, the new menu items feature new cranberry sauce and new turkey stuffing made with parsley, rosemary, sage, breadcrumbs, and broth.
Subway's Festive Feast collection includes the following:
- TurHamKen - A much simpler twist on turducken (a chicken stuffed inside a duck stuffed inside a turkey and then roasted) that consists of roasted turkey, Black Forest ham, and rotisserie-style chicken, served with Monterey Cheddar cheese, spinach, red onions, mayo, stuffing, and cranberry sauce, on your choice of bread.
- Festive Turkey - It's the same as the TurHamKen, only minus the ham and chicken.
- Festive Chicken - Again, it's the same as the TurHamKen, but minus the turkey and ham.
There's also an option to add cranberry sauce or stuffing to any Subway sub for an additional $1.
If you're looking to save some money while trying the new subs, Subway has an online-only, buy-one-get-one-for-one-dollar deal on Footlongs exclusively for its MVP Rewards members when they use the code "FOOTLONGFAN".
Photo via Subway.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Subway Introduces New Festive Feast Subs for Thanksgiving

articles.description:
Denny's is offering its rewards members a free Original Grand Slam redeemable from now through Monday, November 10, 2025, at participating locations nationwide.

The freebie is part of a promotion that promised a free Grand Slam for the first grand slam hit during this year's World Series, i.e., you get a Grand Slam when they hit a grand slam (a home run when the bases are loaded). It can be claimed when ordering online through the Denny's website or mobile app, or by visiting a participating location and using your Denny's Rewards.
Denny's Original Grand Slam is a breakfast platter that includes two buttermilk pancakes, two eggs, two strips of applewood-smoked bacon, and two pork sausage links.
Photo via Denny's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Denny's Offers Free Grand Slam to Rewards Members Through November 10, 2025

articles.description:
Wendy's celebrates the 2025 holiday season with the launch of its new Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion.

Available for a limited time, the Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion features a Chocolate or Vanilla Frosty blended with a cinnamon-y snickerdoodle sauce and topped with snickerdoodle cookie crumbles.
It can also be ordered as a Frosty Swirl, which consists of a Chocolate or Vanilla Frosty swirled with snickerdoodle sauce.
Prices vary, but at my local Wendy's here in Los Angeles, the Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion starts at $4.19 for a small, while the Snickerdoodle Frosty Swirl costs $2.99 for a small.
Photo via Wendy's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Wendy's Launches New Snickerdoodle Frosty Fusion

articles.description:
Shake Shack has brought back its Chicken Sunday deal, where you can get a free Chicken Shack sandwich with an online purchase of $10 or more on Sundays, through the end of the year. It is also offering a buy-one-get-one deal on the Smoke Shack burger throughout November.

The Chicken Sundays deal can be redeemed when ordering through an in-restaurant kiosk, the Shake website, or the Shake Shack mobile app on a Sunday through December 28, 2025, using the code "CHICKENSUNDAY". The Smoke Shack November BOGO deal is also available using the same ordering options, only using the code "SMOKESHACK" instead.
Shake Shack's Chicken Shack is the chain's fried chicken sandwich. It features a crispy-fried chicken breast filet, lettuce, pickles, and buttermilk herb mayo on a toasted potato bun. Prices vary, but it normally costs $9.49 here in Los Angeles.
The Smoke Shack is a burger that features a quarter-pound Angus beef patty, American cheese, applewood-smoked bacon, chopped cherry peppers, and ShackSauce on a toasted potato bun. It normally costs $9.99 near me.
Photo via Shake Shack.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: "Chicken Sunday" Deal Back at Shake Shack Through End of 2025

articles.description:
Monday, November 3, 2025, is National Sandwich Day, when we celebrate various fillings between two slices of bread. Here are some deals and specials to help save some money and mark the occasion:

- Capriotti's - On November 3, 2025, Capriotti's is offering its rewards members a buy-one-get-one-for-50%-off deal on all turkey subs.
- Firehouse Subs - On November 3, 2025, rewards members can get a free medium Hook & Ladder, Firehouse Meatball, Turkey & Provolone, or Italian sub when they place an order of $15 or more through the Firehouse website or mobile app.
- Jimmy John's - On November 3, rewards members can get a half-priced Picklewich when they order any toasted sandwich using the promo code "TOASTED".
- McAlister's Deli - From November 3 through November 9, 2025, rewards members can take advantage of a buy-one-get-one-for-50%-off deal on sandwiches.
- Panera - On November 3, rewards members can buy a whole sandwich and get a second one for $5 at participating locations.
- Potbelly - On November 3, you can buy one Big or Original sandwich and get an Original sandwich for free using the promo code "BOGO" when ordering through the Potbelly website or mobile app.
- Schlotzsky's - Through November 3, 2025, rewards members can get up to 10 medium sandwiches for $5 each using an offer in their account at participating locations.
Other non-National Sandwich Day deals that can still be used to celebrate the occasion include:
- Pizza Hut - There's the Melt Mondays deal, where you can get any Melt for $4.99 at participating locations. 
- Subway - For a limited time, Subway rewards members can buy a Footlong sub and get a second one for $1 using the code "BOGO1".
Photo via Schlotzsky's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: 2025 National Sandwich Day Deals and Specials

articles.description:
Crumbl launches a new Martha Stewart-themed menu as this week's limited-time special. The menu commemorates the relaunch of Martha Stewart's first book, Entertaining.

Available from November 3 through November 8, 2025, Crumbl's Martha Stewart menu includes:
- New New York Cheesecake - A classic New York-style cheesecake on a graham cracker crust, topped with fresh strawberries.
- New Classic Fudge Brownie - A fudge brownie topped with a chocolate glaze and white chocolate drizzle.
- New Martha Stewart's Chocolate Chip Cookie Thins - Crisp cookies with caramelized edges, chewy centers, and semi-sweet chocolate chips.
- New Coconut Layer Cake - A two-tier white cake with layers of vanilla mousse, topped with toasted coconut.
- New Pumpkin Sheet Cake - A spiced pumpkin sheet cake topped with vanilla bean cream cheese frosting and a buttery brown sugar streusel.
- New Mile-High Lemon Pie - Lemon filling in a shortbread crust, topped with whipped cream and a fresh lemon slice.
- New Chocolate Tart Cookie - A chilled chocolate cookie crust filled with fudge and chocolate mousse, topped with whipped cream and dark chocolate curls.
In addition to this week's limited-time menu, Crumbl continues to offer its Secret Menu, which features a limited-time cake or cookie selected by each location. You can check the Crumbl website and mobile app to see what's available near you.
Photo via Crumbl.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Crumbl Debuts New Martha Stewart Menu

articles.description:
Panera debuts its new Frozen Signature Hot Chocolate and brings back its Signature Hot Chocolate for the 2025 holiday season. Both beverages are available now exclusively for MyPanera rewards members ahead of nationwide availability on November 5, 2025.

Panera's Frozen Signature Hot Chocolate features half & half blended with a bittersweet chocolate sauce and ice, topped with whipped cream. In comparison, the chain's Signature Hot Chocolate features steamed milk with the same chocolate sauce, topped with whipped cream.
To kick off the holiday season, on November 5, 2025, Panera will be giving a free Hot Chocolate Plush Keychain to the first 25 in-cafe (at the cashier) purchases of a Signature Hot Chocolate or Frozen Signature Hot Chocolate at participating locations.
Photo via Panera.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Panera Pours New Frozen Signature Hot Chocolate

articles.description:
Taco Bell is testing new Crispy Chicken Crunchwrap Sliders for a limited time at participating locations in the Knoxville, TN area.

Featuring a snack-sized version of Taco Bell's signature Crunchwrap, each one comes filled with crispy-fried chicken nuggets, a three-cheese blend, pico de gallo, and a choice of Creamy Chipotle or new Sriracha Chile Sauce. 
Crispy Chicken Crunchwrap Sliders are priced at $2.49 each.
Taco Bell has occasionally served up Crunchwrap Sliders since 2014. They were most recently offered in March of this year.
Photo via Taco Bell.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Taco Bell Tests New Crispy Chicken Crunchwrap Sliders

articles.description:
Nissin gets into the Thanksgiving spirit this year with the launch of its new Turkey Dinner and Pumpkin Pie Cup Noodles flavors.

The company describes the new flavors as a "moist" solution to the problems of "overcooked turkey, parched pumpkin pie crusts, or dry conversations."
Turkey Dinner Cup Noodles features "rich essence of herb-seasoned turkey broth with sweet corn, tangy cranberry bursts, crisp green beans, warming spices, and tender noodles," while Pumpkin Pie offers a "satisfying pumpkin flavor blended with caramelized brown sugar sweetness and signature pie spices."
The two new seasonal varieties mark the latest in a line of unexpected flavors from Cup Noodles. Others have included Dill Pickle, Campfire S'mores, Everything Bagel, and more.
Turkey Dinner and Pumpkin Pie Cup Noodles are available now for a limited time exclusively at Walmart stores and the Walmart website. They're priced at $1.18 per cup.
Photo via Nissin.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Nissin Releases New Turkey Dinner and Pumpkin Pie Cup Noodles Flavors

articles.description:
Carl's Jr. is testing a new Tri-Tip Burger at participating locations in the Denver area.

The new burger features a charbroiled beef patty, sliced sirloin tri-tip, grilled onions, bistro steak sauce, and a slice of Swiss cheese, served on a toasted brioche bun. Double (two beef patties and two slices of Swiss cheese) and Triple (three patties and three slices of cheese) versions are also available.
The Tri-Tip is definitely on the premium end of the pricing spectrum, starting at $9.99 for the Single version (compared to $6.49 for a Single Famous Star).
Photo via Carl's Jr.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Carl's Jr. Tests New Tri-Tip Burger

articles.description:
Chick-fil-A, under its Red Wagon Ventures subsidiary, has opened a new beverage cafe concept called "Daybright Coffee and Refreshment," featuring a "vibrant mix of crafted beverages and delightful bites that are made in-house daily."

The new cafe features both a dining room and dual drive-thru lanes and is open Monday through Saturday, from 6AM to 9PM. It is located at 4326 Jimmy Lee Smith Parkway in Hiram, GA. 
The Daybright menu includes:


Coffee, including cold brew, protein coffees, and lattes with add-ons like cold foam and syrup.
Iced teas and lemonades.
Fizzes - Sparkling beverages made with fruit juices. 
Smoothies
Bright Pressed Juices - Cold-pressed bottled juice blends.
Daily Donuts - Baked fresh daily with olive oil. 
Portable food, including a warm sandwich, stuffed English muffins, a breakfast burrito, egg bites, and a chocolate pull-apart. 
A kid’s menu that includes a PB&J, egg bites, and smoothies.


The new concept follows on the heels of McDonald's recently shuttered CosMcs cafe concept and Taco Bell's expanding Live Mas Cafe.
Photo via Chick-fil-A.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Chick-fil-A Opens New Beverage Cafe Concept

articles.description:

Regular menu shake flavors include Vanilla, Chocolate, Strawberry, Cookies and Cream, Coffee, Black & White, and Chocolate & Vanilla.
Photo via Shake Shack.

articles.title: Shake Shack Offers $1 Soda, $3 Fries, and $5 Shakes

articles.description:
Expanding on the popularity of its Asian Zensation Zalad (which was made a permanent menu item last year), Zaxbys launches its limited-time Asian Zensation menu, featuring the new Giant Asian Zensation Chicken Finger Wrap and Sweet Asian Glazed Chicken Finger Plate.

The new Giant Asian Zensation Wrap basically takes the Asian Zensation Salad and wraps it up in a warm flour tortilla, i.e., it's a wrap that contains chopped hand-breaded Fried Chicken Fingerz or grilled chicken, mixed greens, carrots, red cabbage, wonton strips, Asian Slaw, and a drizzle of Teriyaki Sauce. The wrap comes with Crinkle Fries and a small drink.
The new Sweet Asian Glazed Chicken Finger Plate includes four, five, or six Chicken Fingerz tossed in a Sweet Asian Glaze, served with coleslaw, Texas toast, Crinkle Fries, and a small drink.
While they don't launch nationally until Monday, November 3, 2025, the new menu items are available now when ordering through the Zaxbys website and mobile app, as well as through third-party delivery platforms.
Additionally, the chain will be adding a new Cookie Butter Milkshake to the menu. Zaxbys' first-ever holiday shake features a vanilla shake blended with cinnamon-spiced syrup and cookie pieces, topped with whipped cream, cookie crumbles, and a maraschino cherry.
Photo via Zaxbys.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Zaxbys Launches New Asian Zensation Menu

articles.description:
Carl's Jr. introduces the new Cali XL burger and new Churro Hand-Scooped Ice-Cream Shake at participating locations chain-wide.

The Cali XL is a larger version of the fast-food chain's value-priced California Classic Double Cheeseburger. It features two charbroiled beef patties, two slices of American cheese, grilled onions, Classic Sauce, lettuce, and tomato on a toasted sesame seed bun. It's priced at $5.99 with the tagline, "Stacked Tall. Priced Small."
In comparison, the similarly appointed California Classic Double Cheeseburger comes with smaller beef patties, only has one slice of American cheese, and is served on a plain bun for $4.99.
The new Churro Hand-Scooped Ice-Cream Shake consists of vanilla ice cream blended with churro syrup, and churro crumbles, crowned with whipped topping and more churro crumbles. Prices vary, but it costs $6.19 at my local Carl's Jr. here in Los Angeles.
Photo via Carl's Jr.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Carl's Jr. Introduces New Cali XL and Churro Shake

articles.description:
Taco Bell is testing a spicy version of its chicken nuggets, called "Diablo Nuggets," for a limited time at participating locations in the Minneapolis area. They've also brought back Loaded Taco Topped Fries for a limited time in the same area.

Diablo Nuggets feature the same white-meat chicken and tortilla-chip breading as the chain's Crispy Chicken Nuggets, only they're dusted with a Diablo Sauce-flavored seasoning for "an extra fiery kick."
The new nuggets are available a la carte in a five-piece order for $4.99 and a 10-piece order for $7.99. You can also get a five-piece Diablo Nuggets combo (with fries and a medium fountain drink) for $6.99 and a 10-piece combo for $9.99
Additionally, there's a Nuggets Deluxe Meal and a $9 Discovery Luxe Box that includes Diablo Nuggets.
The Nuggets Deluxe Meal includes five classic or Diablo Nuggets, a Seasoned Beef Chalupa, a Crunchy Taco, regular Nacho Fries, and a large fountain drink for $11.49.
The $9 Discovery Luxe Box is a box combo that includes five classic or Diablo Nuggets, regular Nacho Fries, a Doritos Locos Taco, Cinnamon Twists, and a medium fountain drink.
Loaded Taco Topped Fries (here's my previous review from 2021) cost $4.99 and feature seasoned fries topped with seasoned beef, shredded lettuce, Spicy Ranch, fiesta strips (crispy red tortilla strips), diced tomatoes, shredded cheddar cheese, and warm nacho cheese sauce.
Photo via Taco Bell.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Taco Bell Tests New Diablo Nuggets

articles.description:
Arby's celebrates Thanksgiving this year with the return of Deep Fried Turkey sandwiches and the debut of its new Apple Pie Shake.

Available for a limited time now, Arby's Deep Fried Turkey lineup includes two options:
- Deep Fried Turkey Gobbler Sandwich (pictured) - A quarter-pound of thick-sliced deep-fried turkey, crispy-fried onions, melted Swiss cheese, stuffing, cranberry spread, and mayo on a toasted sub roll. The seasonal sandwich got some tweaks this year compared to last year's version, which featured a savory herb spread and everything seasoning. The addition of stuffing is new for any Arby's sandwich, and previous renditions came on a round bun.
- Deep Fried Turkey Club Sandwich - A quarter pound of thick-sliced deep-fried Turkey served with bacon, cheddar cheese, tomato, lettuce, and mayo, on a toasted sub roll. 
The new Apple Pie Shake blends the flavor of baked green apples and warming spices (i.e., apple pie-flavored syrup) into Arby's vanilla shake base and is topped with whipped cream and a dusting of cinnamon sugar.
Prices for the Thanksgiving-inspired menu items vary, but the Deep-Fried Turkey Gobbler costs $6.99 at an Arby's here in Los Angeles, the Deep Fried Turkey Club is $6.69, and a regular-size Apple Pie Shake goes for $3.89.
Photo via Arby's.

articles.description:
The other night, as I was sitting in the drive-thru lane at McDonald's to redeem a free Boo Bucket Happy Meal with my rewards points, I recalled the times as a kid when I'd beg my dad to go to McDonald's to get a Happy Meal because of a toy I saw on TV. Then, I thought about how I don't really see my nieces and nephews doing the same. Interestingly, the few times I've seen someone ask for a Happy Meal toy recently, it was an adult trying to get a specific toy (to complete a set, I guess?).

So my question for you guys is, "Have you ever gone to a fast food restaurant primarily to get a toy or other merch (like Coca-Cola glasses or Halloween buckets and the like)?" If you're older, do you feel there's a nostalgia aspect that drives the purchase? Saturday morning cartoons and the Happy Meal commercials that came with had more than a little to do with my want for Happy Meal toys. But that's not something that exists anymore. There's a generational line after which a person did not grow up with Saturday morning cartoons and instead grew up watching YouTube and various on-demand streaming services.
On another note, how do you hear about Happy Meal toys or other kids' meal toys? I learn about them through writing this blog (for over 10 years now!) and from point-of-sale displays when I'm at a restaurant, but I'm curious to learn how it is for others.
As for the Boo Bucket, I plan to fill it with candy and have my nieces and nephews play "Guess how much this cost me?" for it (and then making them share the candy with their siblings anyway...).
Photo via McDonald's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Thoughts for Food: Have You Ever Gone to a Fast Food Restaurant for a Toy?

articles.description:
With Halloween coming up this week, Crumbl welcomes a weekly lineup that includes the new Halloween Confetti Milkshake Cookie, new Chocolate Peanut Butter Brownie featuring Reese's Pieces, and more.

Here are the baked desserts you'll find as part of the limited-time selection at Crumbl from October 27 to November 1, 2025:
- Halloween Confetti Milkshake Cookie - A chilled confetti sugar cookie rolled in white, orange, and black sprinkles and topped with cake-flavored buttercream and a whipped cream rosette.
- Chocolate Peanut Butter Brownie featuring Reese's Pieces - Afudge brownie packed with chopped Reese's Pieces, topped with peanut butter frosting, more chopped Reese's Pieces, and a drizzle of milk chocolate. 
- Dirt Cake Cookie - A warm dark chocolate cookie coated in cookies & cream crumbs, topped with fudge frosting, chocolate streusel, and a gummy worm.
- Cookie Dough Cookie - A chilled brown sugar cookie topped with cookie dough buttercream and cookie dough chunks.
- Pumpkin Cheesecake Cookie - A chilled buttery graham cookie topped with pumpkin cheesecake frosting and graham cracker streusel.
- French Toast Cookie - A buttery toast-shaped cookie topped with a cinnamon egg wash, a dollop of buttercream, a drizzle of syrup, and a dusting of powdered sugar.
- Semi-Sweet Chocolate Chunk Cookie - A warm cookie packed with semi-sweet chocolate chunks and a sprinkle of flaky sea salt.
In addition to the listed cookie, Crumbl is celebrating Halloween with a Graveyard Menu this week. It's a variation of the chain's recent "Secret Menu," where each location will offer a Graveyard flavor that is "making a comeback after years in the Crumbl afterlife." Like the Secret Menu, you can check the Crumbl website and mobile app to see what's available near you.
Photo via Crumbl.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Crumbl Bakes New Halloween Confetti Milkshake Cookie and More Through November 1, 2025
